---
title: "Lowkey"
description: "Self-hosted AI coding agents on AWS — one command, one stack, your account."
---

<Note>
Lowkey deploys an AI coding agent on a fresh EC2 instance in your own AWS account. It creates the VPC, IAM, security services, and runtime; you pick the agent.
</Note>

## What you get

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Your agent, your AWS account" icon="shield-check">
    No shared control plane. The agent runs on EC2 in your account. All state, logs, and secrets stay with you.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Pick any supported agent" icon="boxes-stacked">
    OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, Kiro, NemoClaw, Hermes, Pi, or IronClaw — one installer, one profile, done.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Guardrails on by default" icon="lock">
    Security Hub, GuardDuty, Inspector, Access Analyzer, and Config auto-enable for builder/account_assistant profiles.
  </Card>
  <Card title="CloudFormation or Terraform" icon="code">
    Use whichever IaC your team already runs. Both are kept in sync in the repo.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## One-liner install

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash
```

The installer walks you through **pack**, **profile**, **instance size**, and **deploy method**. For a fully non-interactive install, see [Quickstart](/quickstart).

## Pick a path

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="Quickstart" icon="rocket" href="/quickstart">
    Get an agent running in ~10 minutes.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Pick an agent" icon="robot" href="/agents/overview">
    Compare packs, pick the one that matches your workflow.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Pick a profile" icon="user-shield" href="/profiles/overview">
    Builder, account_assistant, or personal_assistant — AWS blast radius per profile.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Why Lowkey exists

Most agent vendors want you to pipe your code and secrets through their SaaS. Lowkey flips that: the agent is *yours*, running on *your* infra, with *your* IAM and *your* Bedrock bills. The installer just makes that a single command instead of a week of terraform.

If you've already read [this blog post about giving an agent its own AWS account](https://robotpaper.ai/i-gave-my-agent-its-own-aws-account-and-now-it-codes-deploys-and-debugs-full-stack-apps/), Lowkey is the packaged version of what's described there.

## Repository & releases

- Source: [github.com/inceptionstack/lowkey](https://github.com/inceptionstack/lowkey)
- Releases: [github.com/inceptionstack/lowkey/releases](https://github.com/inceptionstack/lowkey/releases)
- Installer URL: `install.lowkey.run`
